engineGuide.h2: Alpha Romeo Mito Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Alpha Romeo Mito across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: The 95PS 1.3 JTDM became the Mito's bestselling diesel, offering strong economy and torque for everyday driving.
commonFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure injectors fail from 70k miles causing rough idle and misfires.
DPF clogging - short-journey use prevents regeneration, triggering limp mode.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup causes poor idle and reduced power.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955B1.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: The lower-output 85PS variant was introduced for improved fuel economy and lower insurance groups.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues - frequent short trips cause blockages and warning lights.
Turbo actuator sticking - wastegate fails leading to loss of boost and power.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955B2.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The range-topping 120PS diesel offered strong performance for the Mito, though less common in UK sales.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise on cold start indicates impending failure.
Injector failure - high-pressure units wear causing rough running and smoke.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955A3.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level 78PS 8V engine provided simple, reliable motoring for budget-conscious Mito buyers.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch - tensioner wear on early units causes rattling and potential valve damage.
Coil pack failure - misfires and rough idle common after 60k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955A1000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The 95PS 16V non-MultiAir offered a balance of performance and simplicity before MultiAir arrived.
commonFailures: Variable valve timing solenoid failure - causes rough idle and reduced power.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear on higher-mileage units leads to blue smoke.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955A1010 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The first MultiAir engine brought variable valve lift to the Mito, improving efficiency and response.
commonFailures: MultiAir unit failure - hydraulic actuator fails causing poor starting and loss of power.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling on cold start indicates impending failure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 940A2.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The turbocharged 135PS MultiAir delivered strong performance while retaining MultiAir efficiency benefits.
commonFailures: MultiAir unit failure - most common cause of poor starting and rough running.
Turbo wastegate sticking - causes loss of boost and check engine light.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955A8.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The flagship 170PS MultiAir Turbo powered the Quadrifoglio Verde, delivering hot-hatch performance.
commonFailures: MultiAir unit failure - high-performance use accelerates hydraulic actuator wear.
Turbocharger wear - high boost pressures cause premature bearing and seal failure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 955A9.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 0.9L Petrol
entries.history: The innovative two-cylinder 85PS TwinAir offered unique character and excellent fuel economy.
commonFailures: Cold start issues - intermittent starting problems linked to MultiAir calibration.
Turbo wastegate failure - actuator sticks causing loss of boost and power.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 199B6.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

entries.title: 0.9L Petrol
entries.history: The higher-output 105PS TwinAir variant delivered more performance while retaining economy benefits.
commonFailures: MultiAir unit calibration issues - causes intermittent starting and rough running.
Water pump failure - premature bearing wear leads to overheating and coolant loss.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 312A3.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Alpha Romeo Mito Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Alpha Romeo Mito's engine range includes the 1.4 MultiAir petrol, 0.9 TwinAir two-cylinder, and 1.3 JTDM diesel - each with known failure patterns that drive replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. This section helps you decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: MultiAir Unit Failure - Why 1.4 MultiAir Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Alpha Romeo Mito Distinctive, Super, Speciale, Veloce (2009-2018, 940A2.000, 955A8.000, 955A9.000)
problems.rootCause: The hydraulic MultiAir actuator unit fails due to oil sludge buildup or internal seal wear, causing loss of variable valve control.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty MultiAir actuator unit, reset ECU parameters, test run.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; unit may fail again if oil maintenance poor.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded MultiAir components, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; addresses root cause permanently.

problems.recommendation: If your Mito has under 80k miles and the MultiAir unit is the only fault, replacement of the unit alone may be cost-effective. For higher-mileage engines or those with additional wear, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 940A2.000 engine prices for your Alpha Romeo Mito

problems.h4: 0.9 TwinAir Starting Problems - Cold Start & MultiAir Issues
problems.affectedModels: Alpha Romeo Mito Progression, Distinctive, Super (2011-2018, 199B6.000, 312A3.000)
problems.rootCause: Intermittent starting issues stem from MultiAir unit calibration drift, spark plug wear, or fuel pressure sensor faults affecting the two-cylinder engine's delicate balance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crank/cam sensors, update ECU software, reset MultiAir parameters.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for intermittent issues; may not resolve mechanical MultiAir faults.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace MultiAir actuator, new gaskets, ECU reset and road test.
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses core cause; recommended if starting issues persist after sensor fixes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TwinAir engine with upgraded components, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage units or repeated MultiAir failures.

problems.recommendation: Start with sensor diagnostics and ECU reset - many TwinAir starting issues resolve with calibration. If problems persist or the MultiAir unit is confirmed faulty, weigh unit replacement against a rebuilt engine based on overall mileage and condition.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 199B6.000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

problems.h4: 1.3 JTDM Injector & DPF Failure - Diesel Common Issues
problems.affectedModels: Alpha Romeo Mito Distinctive, Super, Speciale, Veloce (2009-2018, 955B1.000, 955B2.000)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injectors wear causing poor atomisation and misfires; DPF clogs from short-journey driving preventing regeneration, triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean/test injectors; forced DPF regeneration or chemical clean.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if components not severely worn; may need repeating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit matched set of reconditioned injectors, code to ECU, road test.
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses misfire cause; DPF may still require attention separately.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt JTDM engine with upgraded injectors, DPF-ready, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage diesels with multiple faults.

problems.recommendation: If injector failure is isolated and DPF is healthy, injector replacement offers good value. For cars with combined injector, DPF and turbo issues, a rebuilt engine is often more economical than multiple repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 955B1.000 diesel engine prices for your Alpha Romeo Mito

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Early 1.4 Petrol Engine Risk
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling noise on cold start; if ignored, chain can skip teeth causing valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing, test run.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at first sign of rattle; prevents catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best if chain has already skipped or valve damage suspected.

problems.recommendation: At first sign of timing chain rattle, fit a preventative chain kit - it's far cheaper than engine replacement. If the chain has already failed or valve damage is confirmed, a rebuilt engine is the only viable option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 955A1000 Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbocharger Wastegate Failure - Loss of Boost on Turbo Models
problems.affectedModels: Alpha Romeo Mito Veloce, Speciale, Quadrifoglio Verde (2009-2018, 955A8.000, 955A9.000, 199B6.000)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator sticks or fails due to carbon buildup or mechanical wear, preventing boost control and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, reset ECU, test boost pressure.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if turbo internals are healthy; may not fix internal bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbocharger unit, new gaskets, oil lines checked.
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses core turbo failure; recommended if actuator fix doesn't resolve issue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage performance models with multiple turbo-related faults.

problems.recommendation: Start with actuator replacement if boost loss is the only symptom. If turbo noise, smoke or oil consumption are present, a reconditioned turbo or rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 955A8.000 turbo engine prices for your Alpha Romeo Mito

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Alpha Romeo Mito
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing MultiAir unit failure, TwinAir starting issues, or JTDM injector problems, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Alpha Romeo Mito specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Alpha Romeo Mito Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Alpha Romeo Mito and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Alpha Romeo Mito Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mito's 2009-2018 UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Mito launched in the UK with 1.4 petrol and 1.3/1.6 JTDM diesel engines, establishing the core powertrain lineup.
keyChanges: UK market launch with 1.4 8V 78PS, 1.4 16V 95PS petrol and 1.3 JTDM 95PS, 1.6 JTDM 120PS diesel
Introduction of Alfa DNA drive mode selector across range
Early production focused on Distinctive and Progression trim levels
knownFor: Simple, robust early engines with good parts availability
Timing chain tensioner wear emerging on early 1.4 non-MultiAir units
Strong diesel torque making 1.3 JTDM popular for high-mileage drivers
Limited MultiAir availability in early UK models
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2010 Alpha Romeo Mito replacement engines
years.ticker: Mito 1.4 timing chain rattle • 1.3 JTDM injector failure • early diesel DPF issues

years.description: MultiAir technology arrived on 1.4 petrol engines alongside the innovative 0.9L TwinAir two-cylinder turbo, transforming the Mito's petrol lineup.
keyChanges: MultiAir variable valve lift introduced on 1.4L petrol engines (105PS, 135PS, 170PS)
0.9L TwinAir two-cylinder turbo petrol launched (85PS, later 105PS)
Facelift updates to interior and exterior styling
popularModels: Alpha Romeo Mito Super 1.4 MultiAir Turbo
Alpha Romeo Mito Quadrifoglio Verde 1.4 MultiAir Turbo
knownFor: MultiAir unit failure emerges as dominant replacement driver on 1.4 petrol variants
TwinAir cold start issues and intermittent starting problems reported by owners
Strong enthusiast interest in Quadrifoglio Verde performance variant
Diesel DPF regeneration concerns grow with urban driving patterns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2013 Alpha Romeo Mito replacement engines
years.ticker: MultiAir unit failure • TwinAir cold start issues • QV turbo wastegate problems

years.description: The mid-life facelift brought styling updates, improved infotainment and refined engine calibrations across the Mito range.
keyChanges: Exterior facelift with new front bumper, grille and lighting
Engine management updates for improved emissions and drivability
Trim rationalisation: MiTo, Super, Speciale, Veloce lineup
mainEngines: 955A8.000 - 1.4L MultiAir Turbo petrol 135PS (with TCT auto option)
popularModels: Alpha Romeo Mito Super 1.4 MultiAir Turbo TCT
Alpha Romeo Mito Veloce 1.4 MultiAir Turbo 170
knownFor: TCT dual-clutch transmission option adds complexity but improves performance feel
MultiAir unit failures continue to drive replacement enquiries on high-mileage units
TwinAir 105PS variant offers better performance but retains cold start sensitivity
Diesel variants benefit from Euro 6 updates but DPF concerns remain for urban drivers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2016 Alpha Romeo Mito replacement engines
years.ticker: TCT transmission issues • MultiAir hydraulic unit replacement • TwinAir 105PS calibration problems

years.description: The Mito's final UK production years focused on Euro 6 compliance and special editions before discontinuation in 2018.
keyChanges: Final Euro 6 emissions updates across diesel and petrol engines
Special editions: SBK, Racer, Collezione with unique styling
Production winding down ahead of 2018 discontinuation
Focus on clearing remaining stock with competitive pricing

faq.h2: Alpha Romeo Mito Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Used Alpha Romeo Mito engines typically cost £800-£1,700 supply-only, while rebuilt units range £1,400-£3,200 depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour.
keyPoints: 1.4 8V/16V petrol engines: £1,200-£2,100 rebuilt supply-only
1.4 MultiAir petrol: £1,800-£2,800 rebuilt due to complex hydraulic unit
1.3 JTDM diesel: £1,400-£2,300 rebuilt, injector/DPF considerations
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£800 depending on workshop
items.cta: Compare Alpha Romeo Mito engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Alpha Romeo Mito engine reliable?
items.answer: Mito engines are generally robust with proper maintenance, but the 1.4 MultiAir petrol and 0.9 TwinAir have known issues with the MultiAir hydraulic unit causing starting and running problems.
keyPoints: 1.4 8V/16V non-MultiAir petrol engines are simple and reliable with timing chain attention
1.3 JTDM diesel is durable but injectors and DPF need monitoring on high-mileage cars
MultiAir unit failure is the most common costly fault on 1.4 MultiAir petrol variants
Regular oil changes with correct specification are critical for MultiAir longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable rebuilt Alpha Romeo Mito engines

items.question: What is a MultiAir engine problem on the Mito?
items.answer: The MultiAir hydraulic actuator unit can fail due to oil sludge or seal wear, causing poor starting, rough running, loss of power and check engine lights on 1.4 MultiAir petrol Mito engines.
keyPoints: Symptoms include hard starting, misfires, loss of power and engine warning lights
Failure typically occurs between 60,000-100,000 miles with poor oil maintenance
Unit replacement costs £800-£1,300 specialist, but rebuilt engine may offer better value
Using correct 5W40 C3 specification oil and regular changes reduces failure risk
items.cta: Compare rebuilt MultiAir engine prices for your Alpha Romeo Mito

items.question: Do you supply and fit Alpha Romeo Mito engines?
items.answer: Yes - our UK network of Alpha Romeo Mito specialists offers both supply-only and full supply & fit engine replacement services with nationwide collection and delivery options available.
keyPoints: Supply-only: engine delivered to your chosen garage for fitting
Supply & fit: complete replacement service at vetted specialist workshop
Nationwide coverage with collection from your location available
All options include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Find Alpha Romeo Mito engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: How long does a Mito engine replacement take?
items.answer: A typical Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement takes 2-4 days for supply & fit at a specialist workshop, depending on engine code, variant complexity and parts availability.
keyPoints: Simple 1.4 8V petrol swaps often complete in 2 days
MultiAir and TwinAir variants may require 3-4 days for ECU coding and testing
Diesel variants with DPF/AdBlue systems need additional calibration time
Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 24-48 hours of order confirmation
items.cta: Get a timeline quote for your Alpha Romeo Mito engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with a Mito engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Alpha Romeo Mito specialists, with some suppliers offering extended 24-month coverage.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects and premature failure
Some suppliers offer optional extended warranty for additional cost
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Alpha Romeo Mito engine quotes

items.question: Can I fit a 1.4 MultiAir in a 1.4 8V Mito?
items.answer: Yes, a 1.4 MultiAir engine can physically fit a 1.4 8V Mito chassis, but requires ECU, wiring loom and sensor changes plus specialist coding - not recommended without expert guidance.
keyPoints: Physical mounting points are compatible across 1.4L petrol Mito engines
ECU, wiring harness and sensor differences require significant modification
Specialist coding and calibration essential for MultiAir system operation
Cost of conversion often exceeds value of buying correct engine for variant
items.cta: Enquire about engine compatibility for your Alpha Romeo Mito variant

items.question: Is the 0.9 TwinAir a good engine?
items.answer: The 0.9L TwinAir offers unique character and excellent economy, but has known cold start issues and MultiAir unit sensitivity - best suited to owners who understand its maintenance needs.
keyPoints: Two-cylinder turbo design delivers fun character and 60+ mpg potential
Intermittent cold start issues linked to MultiAir calibration and spark timing
MultiAir unit failure risk similar to larger 1.4 MultiAir engines
Regular servicing with correct oil specification critical for longevity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt TwinAir engine prices for your Alpha Romeo Mito

items.question: What oil does a Mito MultiAir need?
items.answer: Alpha Romeo Mito MultiAir engines require 5W40 C3 specification fully synthetic oil to protect the hydraulic actuator unit - using incorrect oil accelerates MultiAir unit failure.
keyPoints: 5W40 C3 fully synthetic oil is mandatory for all MultiAir engines
Oil changes every 10,000 miles or 12 months maximum recommended
Using incorrect oil specification voids MultiAir unit warranty coverage
Check oil level regularly - low oil accelerates MultiAir hydraulic system wear
items.cta: Find correct oil and service parts for your Alpha Romeo Mito MultiAir

items.question: How to check Mito engine code?
items.answer: Your Alpha Romeo Mito engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox flange, listed on the V5C registration document, and can be verified instantly by entering your reg number above.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block: visible near gearbox connection point
V5C registration document lists engine code in section D.2
Service history and dealer records often include engine code reference
Enter your reg number above for instant DVLA-verified engine code identification
items.cta: Identify your exact Alpha Romeo Mito engine code instantly
